message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: I'm adding opt-in debug/trace logging to StreamListener (start/end detection, flush, reset, emit chunk). Only touching streaming_listener.py. What file(s) does your feature need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: I'm implementing idle timeout/tick() in streaming_listener.py only. Guessing you own messages.py for is_last_chunk feature. Let me know if you need streaming_listener.py too.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: My feature (debug/trace logging) is also confined to streaming_listener.py, so we both need it - no other file works. Since it's shared, let's minimize overlap: I'll only touch StreamListener.__init__ (adding debug=False, debug_logger=None as new trailing kwargs) and add guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls inside existing methods (start-detection branch, rolling end-check branch, emit-chunk point, flush(), and reset()) without restructuring control flow or renaming anything. I won't add a tick()/idle-timeout mechanism or touch method signatures beyond __init__. Please do the same on your end (add your idle-timeout state/params in __init__ as trailing kwargs too, and keep your tick() logic additive) so git merge can combine both sets of additions cleanly. Sound OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Our features both touch StreamListener and can't be split into disjoint files. Let's both proceed on streaming_listener.py but keep edits localized/additive to minimize conflicts: I'm only touching __init__ (new idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts init), adding a timestamp update at the top of receive() for non-empty chunks, and adding a new self-contained tick() method at the end of the class. I won't touch flush() or the core start/end detection logic. Whoever merges second (per the git protocol) will reconcile via git merge, keeping both sets of changes. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed. Proceeding with debug/debug_logger init params + guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls in start-detection, rolling end-check, emit-chunk, flush(), and reset(). Won't touch receive() timestamp logic or add tick(). Will merge via git as per protocol.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed. I'll add idle_timeout_s: float | None = None as a trailing kwarg in __init__, init self._last_chunk_ts = None, add a timestamp update at the top of receive() for non-empty chunks (won't touch your logging lines), and add a new standalone tick(now_ts=None) method at the end of the class. No signature changes elsewhere, no renames. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished: added idle_timeout_s param, self._last_chunk_ts tracking in receive(), and a new tick(now_ts=None) method at the end of the class that emits StreamResponse(chunk='') on timeout and unconditionally resets state. Didn't touch flush() or your logging areas. Existing tests pass.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished: added debug/debug_logger init params and guarded DEBUG-level trace logs at start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush(), and reset paths. Merged your idle_timeout_s/tick() work in cleanly (two small conflicts in __init__ signature/docstring and end of flush()/start of tick() - resolved by keeping both). All streaming tests pass. patch.txt rebuilt from merged tree.
